headline: Johor Illegal Waste Dumps Number 847 Active Sites
context: Johor's state environment committee revealed in March 2026 that 847 active illegal waste dumping sites have been identified statewide, an increase of 34% from 2022.

cards.big: Johor has 847 active illegal dump sites.
cards.sub: Local authorities cleared 312 sites in 2024. In the same year, 420 new sites appeared.

cards.big: Illegal dumping saves operators an estimated RM340 per tonne.
cards.sub: Legal disposal at licensed facilities costs RM480 per tonne. The price gap is the incentive.

cards.big: Only 3 of Johor's 14 local authorities have dedicated waste enforcement teams.
cards.sub: The remaining 11 rely on general enforcement officers with no waste-specific training.

cards.big: Leachate from 23 dump sites has contaminated groundwater used by kampung residents.
cards.sub: DOE identified heavy metals exceeding safe limits at 8 locations.

cards.big: When 420 new dumps appear while 312 are cleared, the system is losing. The incentive structure is broken.
Illegal dumping is an economic problem disguised as an enforcement problem. Close the price gap between legal and illegal disposal, and the 847 sites shrink.
